status: DRAFT — NOT PUBLISHED — SME REVIEW REQUIRED
note: DRAFT end-of-course assessment authored for RA-7001. Every question is grounded in this course's newly authored lesson bodies (data/wordpress-export/courses.json) and paraphrases/applies ANSI/IICRC S520:2024 with clause citations — it does not reproduce the Standard's copyrighted text. Australian English, metric units and AS/NZS context. sourceRef is a review aid only and is not consumed by the seed script. DO NOT seed or publish until a qualified subject-matter expert has reviewed both the course content and these questions for technical accuracy. Structured identically to data/seed/floor-care-quizzes.json. This course carries no IICRC CECs (pending IICRC approval).

questions.questionText: S520:2024 §1 sets out five principles for every remediation. Which list states them correctly?
options: Test, spray, seal, paint, invoice
Provide for safety and health; document; control contamination; remove contamination; prevent recontamination
Contain, sample, disinfect, deodorise, verify
Inspect, quote, demolish, rebuild, guarantee

questions.questionText: Why does physically removing mould-affected porous material take priority over treating it in place?
options: Because chemicals are more expensive than demolition
Because S520 principle §1.4 is contamination removal — remediation physically removes the contamination rather than treating it in situ
Because insurers will not pay for cleaning
Because antimicrobials are banned in Australia

questions.questionText: For respiratory protection on a residential mould job in Australia, which standards govern selection/use and the device itself?
options: OSHA 1910 and NIOSH 42 CFR 84
AS/NZS 1715 (selection, use, maintenance) and AS/NZS 1716 (the respiratory device)
AS/NZS 4849.1 and AS 1668.2
AS/NZS 3760 and AS 3000

questions.questionText: What is the purpose of running an air filtration device to create negative air pressure inside a containment?
options: To dry the structure faster
To keep contaminated air moving into the work area rather than escaping into clean parts of the home
To heat the work area for the technicians
To satisfy the insurer's paperwork only

questions.questionText: Under Australian WHS law, how must risks on a mould job be managed?
options: Only if the client requests it
So far as is reasonably practicable, following the hierarchy of controls
By transferring all liability to the occupant
By relying on PPE alone with no risk assessment
questions.sourceRef: Course module 2; WHS Act 2011 and Regulations

questions.questionText: Which cleaning approach does S520:2024 §9.3.8 support for salvageable non-porous surfaces and framing?
options: Painting over the mould with a sealer
HEPA vacuuming to capture residue, followed by damp wiping with detergent solution
Dry brushing the mould off into the room air
Fogging the room with bleach and leaving it sealed

questions.questionText: What is the correct view of antimicrobials and fungicidal/mould-resistant coatings under S520:2024?
options: They replace the need for physical removal
They have a limited, defined role and are never a substitute for physical removal or moisture correction
They should be applied to every surface as the primary treatment
They permanently prevent all future mould regardless of moisture

questions.questionText: A reverse-cycle split system served the affected room. Following S520:2024 §10.2, what should you do?
options: Run it on high to dry the room quickly
Isolate or shut it down during works, protect/seal returns and supplies, and assess whether it is contaminated
Ignore it — HVAC is out of scope for mould work
Remove it and dispose of it in every case

questions.questionText: Principle §1.5 is 'prevent recontamination'. In practice, how is it delivered?
options: By applying two coats of fungicidal paint
By correcting the moisture source and drying the structure to a stable, dry state, verified with moisture readings
By running an ozone generator overnight
By sealing the room permanently
That the invoice has been paid
That the work area has been returned to Condition 1
That no spores exist anywhere in the building
That the containment was the correct colour

questions.questionText: When independent post-remediation verification is used, who should carry it out?
options: The technician who performed the remediation
An Indoor Environmental Professional who did not perform the remediation, providing an arm's-length clearance
The occupant, using a home test kit
Anyone, provided they take a photo

questions.questionText: Which document set best reflects the S520:2024 §6.3 expectation for closing out a residential job?
options: A single after photo
Scope, before/after photos, moisture logs, equipment used, any deviations with rationale, and the verification result
A verbal summary to the occupant only
The insurer's claim number written on the invoice
